reasons_for: Current rule 'meta text 0' is correct in principle but ambiguous in execution—'로 명시' doesn't clearly state where the guidance label goes, leading to meta-text-contamination. Proposed wording explicitly directs labels outside the block (위/아래), directly preventing the C3 critical defect found in cl-s1.
The identified defect is real and reproducible: guidance text was included in the copy-paste block, violating the 'meta text 0' principle. Clarification is necessary.
Proposed wording preserves the principle while eliminating the ambiguity that caused misapplication.
reasons_against: The current rule already states 'meta text 0'—this is a clarification, not a policy change. A careful reading of the existing rule should have prevented the error. The change adds procedural detail but doesn't fix a conceptual flaw.
The proposed wording is longer and more prescriptive ('위/아래 라벨 명시') when a shorter fix would work: simply change line 33's '로 명시' to '블록 밖에 명시' or '블록 밖(위/아래)에 라벨로 명시'.
suggested_modification: Shorter alternative: revise line 33 to: '단독 블록(코드펜스 등으로 감싸 블록 밖에 "↓ 이 안만 지원서에 붙여넣으세요 ↓"로 명시)으로 제시. 문항별 또는 단일 레터, **메타 텍스트 0**.' OR split into two sentences: '단독 블록(코드펜스)으로 제시. 본문만 넣고, 글자수·안내 문구는 블록 밖(위/아래 라벨)에 둔다. 메타 텍스트 0.' This avoids the nested parentheses in the proposed version while keeping the clarity.
